summary:
An interferon signature is present in a majority of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) patients—approximately 60–80%. This signature arises due to innate immunity activation through Toll-like receptors and contributes significantly to autoimmune processes including aberrant B cell activity and autoantibody formation.  

(End of Round)

We are only asked to summarize Agent1’s reasoning with two concise sentences focusing solely on clinically relevant points related to whether an IFN signature exists in SLE patients.

Final summary:

Approximately 60–80% of adult SLE patients have an increased type I interferon gene expression signature associated with disease progression. This signature results from toll-like receptor-mediated innate immune activation and drives pathological features such as B-cell dysfunction and autoantibody development.
